title: Find the Maximum Element
description:
Given a list of integers `nums`, write a function `find_max(nums)` that returns the largest integer in the list. Do not use the built-in `max()` function if you want to challenge yourself (though it is allowed).

**Example:**
`find_max([1, 5, 3, 9, 2])` should return `9`.

title: Fix the Average Calculator
description:
The function below is supposed to calculate the average of a list of numbers, but it contains a bug.

```python
def calculate_average(numbers):
    total = 0
    for num in numbers:
        total += num
    return total / len(numbers) - 1
```

**Task 1:** Predict what this buggy code will output for `calculate_average([10, 20, 30])`.
**Task 2:** Fix the code so it correctly calculates the average.
rubric: The student should predict that the output will be 19.0 (or 19) because 60 / 3 - 1 = 19. They should identify the `- 1` as the error.

title: Time Complexity of Binary Search
description: What is the worst-case time complexity of finding an element in a sorted array using the Binary Search algorithm?

title: List Comprehension Output
description:
What is the output of the following Python code?

```python
nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
result = [x * 2 for x in nums if x % 2 != 0]
print(result)
```

title: Explain Recursion
description: Explain the concept of recursion in programming. Include the two essential components required for a recursive function to work correctly and avoid infinite loops. Provide a simple pseudocode example.
rubric: The student must explain that recursion is when a function calls itself. They MUST identify the two key components: 1) A base case (termination condition) and 2) A recursive step (moving towards the base case). The pseudocode should demonstrate these two parts.

title: Server Load Balancing
description:
You have a web application receiving 10,000 requests per minute. You have 4 servers behind a load balancer using a strict Round-Robin algorithm. Server A has 4 CPU cores, Server B has 8 cores, Server C has 2 cores, and Server D has 4 cores.

Assuming all requests require the same amount of processing power, which server is most likely to experience high CPU utilization (bottleneck) first?

title: Problem Scoping Stage
description: What is the purpose of defining the problem statement during the Problem Scoping stage in an AI project cycle?

options.text: To understand the aim and objective of the project

title: Categorization of AI Models
description: In what ways can AI models be categorized based on the type of data fed into them?

title: Function of Statistical Data
description: In Statistical Data, what is the primary function of the system in relation to data?

title: Goal of Computer Vision
description: What is the main goal of Computer Vision projects?

options.text: Translating audio data into visual descriptions
Converting digital data into analogue signals
Teaching machines to understand textual information
Converting digital visual data into computer-readable language

title: Focus of NLP
description: What is the primary focus of NLP?

options.text: Interacting between computers and humans using artificial language
Dealing with the interaction between computers and humans using natural language

title: Steps in AI Project Cycle
description: Outline the main steps in the AI Project Cycle briefly.
rubric: The student must state the 6 stages of the AI project cycle: 1) Problem Scoping, 2) Data Acquisition, 3) Data Exploration, 4) Modelling, 5) Evaluation, and 6) Deployment.

title: Value-based Frameworks
description: How do value-based frameworks contribute to ethical decision-making by emphasizing fundamental principles and values?
rubric: The student should explain that value-based frameworks focus on moral philosophies and assess the moral worth of actions, typically categorized into rights-based, utility-based, and virtue-based approaches.
